macro_segments.title: New Mishna: The Creditor's Acceptance of Invalid Witnesses or Judges and the Dispute Over Retraction Rights

Shmuel's Distinction: Dispute Over Creditor's Forgiveness Versus Debtor's Payment Acceptance

macro_segments.title: Rava's Proof and the Three-Sided Dispute: When Retraction is Permitted

micro_segments.title: Rava's Teaching on the Oath Case: Distinguishing Between Dispute in Payment and Dispute in Forgiveness

Resolution: Distinguishing Between Nishba'in ve-Lo Meshalmin and Nishba'in ve-Notlin Cases

macro_segments.title: Reish Lakish's Temporal Distinction: Dispute Location Before Versus After Judgment is Finalized

micro_segments.title: Reish Lakish's Position: Dispute Exists Before Judgment; All Agree No Retraction After Judgment
Rabbi Yochanan's Counterpoint: Dispute Continues Even After Judgment is Rendered
Rava's Proof: Accepting Unfit Witnesses Before Judgment Allows Retraction; After Judgment Does Not
Raised Question: Does the Dispute Extend to Before Judgment or Only Occur After?
Conclusion: According to Rabbi Yochanan and Rava, Dispute Exists Only After Judgment is Final

micro_segments.title: Letter Exchange Between Rav Nachman bar Rav Chisda and Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak: Dispute Location
Reply: Dispute is After Judgment and Halakha Follows Chachamim (No Retraction After Judgment)
Rav Ashi's Alternative Question: Is Dispute in Debtor's Payment or Creditor's Forgiveness?
Rav Ashi's Answer: Dispute is in Debtor's Payment Case; Halakha Follows Chachamim
